children.heading: .—Meetings
children.label: a .—Meetings
children.text: The Committee shall, not less than once each year, meet at the call of the Commission or a majority of the members of the Committee.
Chapter 81 of title 5.
Chapter 171 of title 28 and any other Federal law relating to tort liability.
Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a member of the Committee may serve on such committee on a voluntary basis without pay.
No member of the Committee shall receive compensation for service on the Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: the member is authorized to represent the interests of the applicable entity or group; and

children.label: B
children.text: requirements under Federal law that would interfere with such representation and that apply to a special Government employee (as defined in ), including requirements relating to employee conduct, political activities, ethics, conflicts of interest, and corruption, do not apply to the member.section 202(a) of title 18

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: 2 .—Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (b), a member of the Committee shall be treated as a special Government employee for purposes of the committee service of the member if the member, without regard to service on the Committee, is a special Government employee.

children.heading: .—Solicitation of nominations
children.label: 1 .—Solicitation of nominations
children.text: Before appointing an individual as a member of the Committee, the Commission shall publish a timely notice in the Federal Register soliciting nominations for membership on such Committee.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: After considering nominations received pursuant to a notice published under paragraph (1), the Commission may appoint a member to the Committee.

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: B .—Prohibition
children.text: The Commission shall not seek, consider, or otherwise use information concerning the political affiliation of a nominee in making an appointment to the Committee.

children.heading: .—Service at pleasure of commission
children.label: 3 .—Service at pleasure of commission
children.text: Each member of the Committee shall serve at the pleasure of the Commission.

children.heading: .—Security background examinations
children.label: 4 .—Security background examinations
children.text: The Commission may require an individual to have passed an appropriate security background examination before appointment to the Committee.

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: 5 .—Prohibition
children.text: A Federal employee may not be appointed as a member of the Committee.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term of each member of the Committee shall expire on December 31 of the third full year after the effective date of the appointment.

children.heading: .—Continued service after term
children.label: B .—Continued service after term
children.text: When the term of a member of the Committee ends, the member, for a period not to exceed 1 year, may continue to serve as a member until a successor is appointed.

children.heading: .—Vacancies
children.label: 7 .—Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy on the Committee shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: .—Special rule for reappointments
children.label: 8 .—Special rule for reappointments
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2), the Commission may reappoint a member of a committee for any term, other than the first term of the member, without soliciting, receiving, or considering nominations for such appointment.

children.heading: .—Staff Services
children.label: f .—Staff Services
children.text: The Commission shall furnish to the Committee any staff and services considered by the Commission to be necessary for the conduct of the Committee’s functions.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Committee shall elect a Chair and Vice Chair from among the committee’s members.

children.heading: .—Vice chairman acting as chairman
children.label: 2 .—Vice chairman acting as chairman
children.text: The Vice Chair shall act as Chair in the absence or incapacity of, or in the event of a vacancy in the office of, the Chair.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Chair of the Committee may establish and disestablish subcommittees and working groups for any purpose consistent with the function of the Committee.

children.heading: .—Participants
children.label: 2 .—Participants
children.text: Subject to conditions imposed by the Chair, members of the Committee may be assigned to subcommittees and working groups established under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Consultation, Advice, Reports, and Recommendations.—
children.label: i Consultation, Advice, Reports, and Recommendations.—

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: 1 .—Consultation
children.text: Before taking any significant action, the Commission shall consult with, and consider the information, advice, and recommendations of, the Committee if the function of the Committee is to advise the Commission on matters related to the significant action.

children.heading: .—Advice, reports, and recommendations
children.label: 2 .—Advice, reports, and recommendations
children.text: The Committee shall submit, in writing, to the Commission its advice, reports, and recommendations, in a form and at a frequency determined appropriate by the Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: publish the recommendations on a public website; and

children.label: B
children.text: respond, in writing, to the Committee regarding the recommendations, including by providing an explanation of actions taken regarding the recommendations.

children.heading: .—Submission to congress
children.label: 4 .—Submission to congress
children.text: The Commission shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate the advice, reports, and recommendations received from the Committee under paragraph (2).
attend any meeting of the Committee; and
participate as an observer at such meeting.

children.heading: .—Termination
children.label: k .—Termination
children.text: The Committee shall terminate on .September 30, 2029
